document.diagnosis: Iteration 21 favored answer-generating review: the retained five-bank three-review champion improved base plurality from 3/12 to 8/12 through six helpful interventions, one harmful reversal, and two correct logic answers absent from the base bank. Its 50% weakest-family accuracy also exceeded every alternative. Candidate selection remains unresolved: the pipeline now has 41/78 expanded-oracle coverage but only 31/78 exact, while its cumulative correction balance remains favorable at 23 helpful versus 3 harmful. The next batch should preserve both mechanisms while testing whether nine-base coverage is better converted by one reviewer or by committee-level selection.
extensions_requested: Add a shared candidate-audit board: independent reviewers record structured support and falsification evidence for each shuffled candidate before a separately registered terminal selector chooses or repairs an answer. This directly targets the expanded-oracle-to-final gap without exposing frequencies or benchmark-specific information.
most_important_evidence: The Iteration 21 champion scored 8/12 with 50% weakest-family accuracy, versus 6/12 for both cross-examination and one-review repair and 5/12 for regenerate-then-repair.
The champion made six helpful corrections and one harmful reversal; importantly, repair created two correct logic answers absent from its five-answer bank.
Across six pipeline panels, regenerate-then-repair is 31/78 exact with 41/78 expanded-oracle coverage versus 32/78 base-oracle coverage, but ten covered cases were not converted to correct finals.
The pipeline's pooled 23 helpful versus 3 harmful interventions supports unchanged replication despite Iteration 21 producing no new correct regenerated candidate.

In Iteration 20, nine-bank one-review repair exposed 10/18 oracle coverage but converted only 5/18, including 0/6 final logic accuracy despite 5/6 logic oracle coverage; selection architecture is therefore the clearest bottleneck.

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the frozen pipeline unchanged. Its pooled regeneration gain of nine additional oracle-covered cases and strongly positive intervention balance remain promising, while another sealed panel will test whether Iteration 21's zero generation gain was panel variance.
proposals.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the operationally equivalent nine-bank one-review mechanism as the close allocation and call-efficiency probe. It tests whether spending calls on independent candidate coverage rather than extra reviewers can overcome the selector gap on a fresh panel.
proposals.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Test a genuinely different selection architecture. Three disjoint committee delegates expose independent support structure to falsifying selectors, while delegate fusion retains one deterministic base-plurality anchor against weak-review reversals.
proposals.name: Committee-Delegate Falsifying Selection with Fusion

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate sequential cross-examination unchanged as a backup orthogonal lane. Its Iteration 21 correction balance was three helpful and zero harmful, so additional evidence can determine whether sequential falsification reduces reversals despite its weaker logic result.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

proposals.hypothesis: Provide a pure deterministic aggregation backup that isolates whether a larger answer bank improves final accuracy without any weak selector or answer-generating review.
proposals.name: Nine-Vote Plurality

research_hypotheses: Regenerate-then-repair's pooled coverage gain is reproducible even though its terminal selector currently leaves ten expanded-oracle cases unconverted.
A single falsifying reviewer over nine raw answers offers a better candidate-generation versus review allocation than three reviewers over five answers.
Committee delegates plus delegate fusion can improve selection stability by preserving independent support structure and anchoring the result against reviewer error.
Sequentially transmitted critiques reduce harmful reversals more reliably than parallel independent review.
Nine-way deterministic plurality distinguishes the benefit of bank expansion from the benefit or harm of learned review.
document.risk_note: The anticipated six-strategy batch—three mechanical retentions plus the first three proposals—costs at most 240 Luna calls: 108 shared bank calls and 132 review calls, below the 300-call cap. Principal risks are fresh-panel variance, committee compression discarding correct minority candidates, and continued weakness on logic. All results remain development evidence, not validation of general transfer.
